FPUS55 KFGZ 090632
ZFPFGZ

Zone Forecast Product for Northern Arizona
National Weather Service Flagstaff AZ
1132 PM MST Mon Jun 8 2026

These forecasts provide average values for large geographical
areas. They may not be representative of the location you are
interested in. For a more specific forecast...please visit 
weather.gov/flagstaff and either (1) select a location from the 
dropdown menu above the map...or (2) click a location on the map. 
you can refine your selection by clicking on the map displayed on 
the resulting page.

AZZ015-091100-
Western Mogollon Rim-
Including the cities of Flagstaff, Williams, and Munds Park
1132 PM MST Mon Jun 8 2026

.REST OF TONIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows 43 to 53. Light winds
becoming south around 15 mph early in the morning. 
.TUESDAY...Windy, sunny. Highs 77 to 85. Southwest winds 20 to
30 mph with gusts to around 45 mph. 
.TUESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows 41 to 49. Southwest winds 15 to
20 mph with gusts to around 35 mph early in the evening becoming
light, then becoming southwest around 15 mph early in the
morning. 
.WEDNESDAY...Sunny. Highs 82 to 90. Light winds becoming
southwest 15 to 20 mph in the afternoon. 
.WEDNESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows 44 to 54. Southwest winds around
15 mph. Gusts up to 30 mph in the evening. 
.THURSDAY...Sunny. Highs 83 to 91. 
.THURSDAY NIGHT AND FRIDAY...Partly cloudy. Lows 45 to 55. Highs
81 to 91. 
.FRIDAY NIGHT...Mostly cloudy in the evening then becoming partly
cloudy. Lows 51 to 61. 
.SATURDAY...Partly cloudy with a chance of rain showers and a
slight chance of thunderstorms. Highs 81 to 89. Chance of
precipitation 30 percent. 
.SATURDAY NIGHT...Partly cloudy with a 10 percent chance of
thunderstorms. Lows 51 to 61. 
.SUNDAY...Mostly sunny in the morning then becoming partly
cloudy. A 20 percent chance of thunderstorms. Highs 81 to 89. 
.SUNDAY NIGHT...Partly cloudy. Lows 50 to 60. 
.MONDAY...Mostly sunny in the morning then becoming partly
cloudy. A 20 percent chance of thunderstorms. Highs 79 to 87. 

$$
=

AZZ006-091100-
Grand Canyon Country-
Including the cities of Grand Canyon Village, Tusayan, Supai, 
and Grand Canyon North Rim
1132 PM MST Mon Jun 8 2026

.REST OF TONIGHT...Partly cloudy. Lows around 56 on the North Rim
to around 64 along the Colorado River. South winds around 15 mph
late in the evening becoming light, then becoming south around
15 mph early in the morning. 
.TUESDAY...Breezy, sunny. Highs around 77 on the North Rim to
around 103 along the Colorado River. Southwest winds 15 to
25 mph. 
.TUESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows around 53 on the North Rim to around
62 along the Colorado River. Southwest winds around 15 mph with
gusts to around 30 mph early in the evening becoming light. 
.WEDNESDAY...Sunny. Highs 91 to 101. Light winds becoming west
around 15 mph in the afternoon. 
.WEDNESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows 53 to 63. Light winds. 
.THURSDAY...Sunny. Highs around 82 on the North Rim to around
109 along the Colorado River. 
.THURSDAY NIGHT AND FRIDAY...Mostly clear. Lows around 57 on the
North Rim to around 66 along the Colorado River. Highs around
82 on the North Rim to around 108 along the Colorado River. 
.FRIDAY NIGHT...Partly cloudy. Lows around 64 on the North Rim to
around 71 along the Colorado River. 
.SATURDAY...Partly cloudy with a 10 percent chance of
thunderstorms. Highs around 81 on the North Rim to around
104 along the Colorado River. 
.SATURDAY NIGHT AND SUNDAY...Mostly clear. A 10 percent chance of
thunderstorms. Lows around 63 on the North Rim to around 70 along
the Colorado River. Highs around 81 on the North Rim to around
104 along the Colorado River. 
.SUNDAY NIGHT AND MONDAY...Mostly clear. Lows around 63 on the
North Rim to around 70 along the Colorado River. Highs around
80 on the North Rim to around 104 along the Colorado River. 

$$
=

AZZ008-091100-
Yavapai County Mountains-
Including the cities of Prescott, Seligman, Ash Fork, 
and Crown King
1132 PM MST Mon Jun 8 2026

.REST OF TONIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows 48 to 58. South winds
around 15 mph. 
.TUESDAY...Sunny. Highs 82 to 88. South winds 15 to 20 mph. Gusts
up to 35 mph in the late morning and afternoon. 
.TUESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows 46 to 56. Southwest winds around
15 mph with gusts to around 30 mph early in the evening becoming
light. 
.WEDNESDAY...Sunny. Highs 87 to 93. Light winds becoming
southwest around 15 mph in the afternoon. 
.WEDNESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows 49 to 59. Southwest winds around
15 mph in the evening becoming light. 
.THURSDAY...Sunny. Highs 88 to 94. 
.THURSDAY NIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows 51 to 61. 
.FRIDAY...Partly cloudy with a 20 percent chance of
thunderstorms. Highs 87 to 93. 
.FRIDAY NIGHT...Partly cloudy with a 10 percent chance of
thunderstorms. Lows 57 to 67. 
.SATURDAY...Partly cloudy with a 20 percent chance of
thunderstorms. Highs 88 to 94. 
.SATURDAY NIGHT AND SUNDAY...Mostly clear. A 10 percent chance of
thunderstorms. Lows 58 to 68. Highs 88 to 94. 
.SUNDAY NIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows 57 to 67. 
.MONDAY...Mostly sunny with a 20 percent chance of thunderstorms.
Highs 86 to 92. 

$$

Yavapai County Valleys and Basins-
Including the cities of Cottonwood, Camp Verde, Cordes Junction,

.REST OF TONIGHT...Clear. Lows 56 to 66. Light winds. 
.TUESDAY...Sunny. Highs 89 to 97. Light winds becoming south

.TUESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows 54 to 64. Southwest winds around
15 mph early in the evening becoming light. 
.WEDNESDAY...Sunny. Highs 94 to 102. Light winds becoming
southwest around 15 mph in the afternoon. 
.WEDNESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows 57 to 67. Light winds. 
.THURSDAY...Mostly sunny. Highs 94 to 102. 
.THURSDAY NIGHT...Partly cloudy. Lows 59 to 69. 
.FRIDAY AND FRIDAY NIGHT...Partly cloudy with a 10 percent chance

Highs 95 to 103. 
.SUNDAY NIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows 65 to 75. 
.MONDAY...Mostly sunny with a 10 percent chance of thunderstorms.
Highs 92 to 100.

Including the cities of Sedona and Village of Oak Creek

.REST OF TONIGHT...Clear. Lows 54 to 62. Light winds. 
.TUESDAY...Breezy, sunny. Highs 85 to 93. Light winds becoming
south 15 to 25 mph in the late morning and afternoon. 
.TUESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows 49 to 57. Southwest winds around
15 mph with gusts to around 30 mph early in the evening becoming
light, then becoming northeast around 15 mph early in the
morning. 
.WEDNESDAY...Sunny. Highs 90 to 98. Light winds becoming

.WEDNESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows 53 to 61. Light winds. 
.THURSDAY...Sunny. Highs 91 to 99.

AZZ005-091100-
Marble and Glen Canyons-
Including the cities of Page and Lees Ferry
1132 PM MST Mon Jun 8 2026 /1232 AM MDT Tue Jun 9 2026/

.REST OF TONIGHT...Mostly clear late in the evening then becoming
mostly cloudy. Lows 59 to 65. Southwest winds around 15 mph late
in the evening becoming light, then becoming south around 15 mph
early in the morning. 
.TUESDAY...Sunny. Highs 90 to 98. Southwest winds 15 to 20 mph. 
.TUESDAY NIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows 56 to 62. West winds 15 to
20 mph in the evening becoming light, then becoming southwest
around 15 mph early in the morning. 
.WEDNESDAY...Sunny. Highs 94 to 102. Light winds becoming west
around 15 mph in the afternoon. 
.WEDNESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows around 62. West winds around
15 mph. 
.THURSDAY...Sunny. Highs 92 to 100. 
.THURSDAY NIGHT AND FRIDAY...Mostly clear. Lows around 61. Highs
92 to 102. 
.FRIDAY NIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows around 68.

thunderstorms. Highs 94 to 100.

.SUNDAY...Mostly sunny with a 10 percent chance of thunderstorms.
Highs 94 to 100. 
.SUNDAY NIGHT AND MONDAY...Mostly clear. Lows around 67. Highs
93 to 100.

AZZ004-091100-
Kaibab Plateau-
Including the cities of Jacob Lake and Fredonia
1132 PM MST Mon Jun 8 2026

.REST OF TONIGHT...Partly cloudy. Lows 49 to 59. Southwest winds
around 15 mph. 
.TUESDAY...Breezy, sunny. Highs 78 to 86 above 6000 feet...84 to
90 below 6000 feet. Southwest winds 15 to 25 mph. Gusts up to
40 mph in the late morning and afternoon. 
.TUESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows 47 to 57. Southwest winds 15 to
20 mph with gusts to around 35 mph in the evening becoming light,
then becoming southwest around 15 mph early in the morning. 
.WEDNESDAY...Sunny. Highs 83 to 93. Light winds becoming west
15 to 20 mph in the afternoon. 
.WEDNESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows 50 to 60. West winds around
15 mph. Gusts up to 30 mph in the evening. 
.THURSDAY AND THURSDAY NIGHT...Clear. Highs 83 to 91 above
6000 feet...90 to 96 below 6000 feet. Lows 50 to 60. 
.FRIDAY AND FRIDAY NIGHT...Mostly clear. Highs 82 to 92 above
6000 feet...90 to 96 below 6000 feet. Lows 53 to 63. 
.SATURDAY...Partly cloudy with a 10 percent chance of
thunderstorms. Highs 83 to 91 above 6000 feet...90 to 96 below
6000 feet. 
.SATURDAY NIGHT AND SUNDAY...Mostly clear. A 10 percent chance of
thunderstorms. Lows 55 to 65. Highs 82 to 91 above 6000 feet...
89 to 96 below 6000 feet. 
.SUNDAY NIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows 55 to 65. 
.MONDAY...Mostly sunny with a 10 percent chance of thunderstorms.
Highs 81 to 89 above 6000 feet...88 to 94 below 6000 feet.

AZZ007-091100-
Coconino Plateau-
Including the city of Valle
1132 PM MST Mon Jun 8 2026 /1232 AM MDT Tue Jun 9 2026/

.REST OF TONIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows around 55 on the plateau to
around 68 along the Colorado River. South winds around 15 mph
late in the evening becoming light, then becoming south around
15 mph early in the morning. 
.TUESDAY...Breezy, sunny. Highs around 82 on the plateau to
around 96 along the Colorado River. Southwest winds 15 to 25 mph.
Gusts up to 40 mph in the late morning and afternoon. 
.TUESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows around 54 on the plateau to around
65 along the Colorado River. Southwest winds around 15 mph with
gusts to around 30 mph early in the evening becoming light. 
.WEDNESDAY...Sunny. Highs 87 to 93. Light winds becoming
southwest 15 to 20 mph in the afternoon. 
.WEDNESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows 48 to 58. Southwest winds around
15 mph in the evening becoming light. 
.THURSDAY...Sunny. Highs around 90 on the plateau to around
104 along the Colorado River. 
.THURSDAY NIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows around 58 on the plateau to
around 70 along the Colorado River. 
.FRIDAY AND FRIDAY NIGHT...Partly cloudy. Highs around 87 on the
plateau to around 101 along the Colorado River. Lows 58 to 64 on
the plateau to 70 to 75 along the Colorado River. 
.SATURDAY...Partly cloudy with a 20 percent chance of
thunderstorms. Highs around 89 on the plateau to around 101 along
the Colorado River. 
.SATURDAY NIGHT...Partly cloudy with a 10 percent chance of
thunderstorms. Lows around 64 on the plateau to around 76 along
the Colorado River. 
.SUNDAY...Mostly sunny with a 10 percent chance of thunderstorms.
Highs around 89 on the plateau to around 101 along the Colorado
River. 
.SUNDAY NIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows around 64 on the plateau to
around 75 along the Colorado River. 
.MONDAY...Mostly sunny with a 10 percent chance of thunderstorms.
Highs around 87 on the plateau to around 100 along the Colorado
River.

.TUESDAY...Windy, sunny. Highs around 90. South winds 15 to

around 45 mph in the afternoon. 
.TUESDAY NIGHT...Breezy. Mostly clear. Lows around 48. Southwest

around 93.

around 92.

.SUNDAY...Mostly sunny. Highs around 92.

.TUESDAY...Windy, sunny. Highs 89 to 95. South winds 15 to 20 mph

30 mph with gusts to around 45 mph in the afternoon. 
.TUESDAY NIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows around 50. Southwest winds

Including the cities of St. Johns and Springerville

.REST OF TONIGHT...Partly cloudy with a chance of sprinkles and a
slight chance of thunderstorms. Lows around 54. Light winds
becoming southeast around 15 mph early in the morning. Chance of
precipitation 10 percent. 
.TUESDAY...Windy. Mostly sunny. Highs 86 to 92. South winds 15 to

20 to 30 mph with gusts to around 45 mph in the afternoon. 
.TUESDAY NIGHT...Breezy, clear. Lows around 49. Southwest winds

15 to 20 mph in the afternoon.

AZZ017-091100-
White Mountains-
Including the cities of Show Low, Greer, and Pinetop-Lakeside
1132 PM MST Mon Jun 8 2026

.REST OF TONIGHT...Partly cloudy with a chance of sprinkles and a
slight chance of thunderstorms. Lows 43 to 51 above 7000 feet...
51 to 57 below 7000 feet. Light winds. Chance of precipitation
10 percent. 
.TUESDAY...Breezy. Mostly sunny. Highs 74 to 82 above 7000 feet...
83 to 89 below 7000 feet. Southwest winds 15 to 25 mph. Gusts up
to 40 mph in the late morning and afternoon. 
.TUESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows 40 to 48 above 7000 feet...49 to
55 below 7000 feet. Southwest winds around 15 mph with gusts to
around 30 mph early in the evening becoming light. 
.WEDNESDAY...Sunny. Highs 78 to 88. Southwest winds 15 to 20 mph.
.WEDNESDAY NIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows 42 to 52. Southwest winds
around 15 mph. 
.THURSDAY...Mostly sunny in the morning then becoming partly
cloudy. Highs 77 to 85 above 7000 feet...around 89 below
7000 feet. 
.THURSDAY NIGHT...Partly cloudy. Lows 43 to 51 above 7000 feet...
52 to 58 below 7000 feet. 
.FRIDAY...Partly cloudy with a 20 percent chance of
thunderstorms. Highs 75 to 83 above 7000 feet...86 to 92 below
7000 feet. 
.FRIDAY NIGHT...Mostly cloudy in the evening then becoming partly
cloudy. Lows 46 to 54 above 7000 feet...around 59 below
7000 feet. 
.SATURDAY...Partly cloudy in the morning then becoming mostly
cloudy. A 40 percent chance of thunderstorms. Highs 74 to
82 above 7000 feet...84 to 90 below 7000 feet. 
.SATURDAY NIGHT...Partly cloudy. Lows 45 to 53 above 7000 feet...
around 58 below 7000 feet. 
.SUNDAY...Mostly sunny in the morning then becoming partly
cloudy. A 20 percent chance of thunderstorms. Highs 74 to
82 above 7000 feet...around 87 below 7000 feet. 
.SUNDAY NIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows 45 to 53 above 7000 feet...
around 57 below 7000 feet. 
.MONDAY...Mostly sunny in the morning then becoming partly
cloudy. A 30 percent chance of thunderstorms. Highs 72 to
80 above 7000 feet...around 85 below 7000 feet. 

$$
=

AZZ018-091100-
Northern Gila County-
Including the cities of Payson, Strawberry, and Young
1132 PM MST Mon Jun 8 2026

.REST OF TONIGHT...Clear. Lows 53 to 63. Light winds. 
.TUESDAY...Sunny. Highs 85 to 93. Light winds becoming southwest
15 to 20 mph in the late morning and afternoon. 
.TUESDAY NIGHT...Clear. Lows 50 to 60. West winds around 15 mph
early in the evening becoming light. 
.WEDNESDAY...Sunny. Highs 88 to 96. Light winds becoming
southwest around 15 mph in the afternoon. 
.WEDNESDAY NIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows 52 to 62. Light winds. 
.THURSDAY...Mostly sunny. Highs 89 to 97. 
.THURSDAY NIGHT...Partly cloudy. Lows 54 to 64. 
.FRIDAY...Partly cloudy with a 10 percent chance of
thunderstorms. Highs 89 to 97. 
.FRIDAY NIGHT...Partly cloudy. Lows 59 to 69. 
.SATURDAY...Partly cloudy in the morning then becoming mostly
sunny. A 10 percent chance of thunderstorms. Highs 89 to 97. 
.SATURDAY NIGHT...Partly cloudy. Lows 59 to 69. 
.SUNDAY...Mostly sunny with a 10 percent chance of thunderstorms.
Highs 88 to 96. 
.SUNDAY NIGHT...Mostly clear. Lows 58 to 68. 
.MONDAY...Mostly sunny with a 20 percent chance of thunderstorms.
Highs 86 to 94. 

$$
=





=====
Thanks for using Winlink, an Amateur Radio Safety Foundation 
sponsored project. For information about Winlink or to manage
your Winlink account please visit: 
https://www.winlink.org

For information on how you can help support Winlink and other
Amateur Radio Safety Foundation projects please visit:
https://www.arsfi.org
